message:
fix(cancel): always propagate cancellation to children and listeners

Change child-cancel and listener-notify paths to use unwrap_or(Time::ZERO)
instead of if-let-Some for the cancelled_at timestamp. This ensures
cancellation always fires even during the brief window where is_cancelled()
returns true but cancelled_at() returns None (between the AtomicBool CAS and
the cancelled_at AtomicU64 store).

Previously, a child registered or listener attached during this exact window
would silently miss the cancellation notification — the token was cancelled
but the child/listener was never told. With the fallback to Time::ZERO, the
cancel signal always propagates; the timestamp may be imprecise (epoch) but
the cancellation itself is never lost.

Affected paths:
- register_child(): cancel newly-registered child if parent already cancelled
- register_listener(): notify newly-registered listener if already cancelled

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
